entry.title: Most Google Cloud Attacks Start With Bug Exploitation
entry.content:
Forget stolen credentials and misconfigurations. Thanks to AI, the new top cause of compromises in the cloud is vulnerability exploits that beat patching cycles.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Will AI Save Consumers From Smartphone-Based Phishing Attacks?
entry.content:
Sophisticated phishing attacks are bypassing on-device protections with troubling frequency, making it more critical than ever for users to protect themselves from potential threats, new research from Omdia shows.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cyberattackers Don't Care About Good Causes
entry.content:
Sightline Security's founder and advisory board discuss how cybersecurity poses significant problems for nonprofits and suggest ways the industry can help.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: The Data Gap: Why Nonprofit Cyber Incidents Go Underreported
entry.content:
Threat actors target nonprofits due to security gaps and highly coveted information, but a lack of sufficient data makes it difficult to grasp the entire picture.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Attackers Abuse LiveChat to Phish Credit Card, Personal Data
entry.content:
A social engineering campaign impersonating PayPal and Amazon uses customer support interactions to acquire sensitive info.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Inside Olympic Cybersecurity: Lessons From Paris 2024 to Milan Cortina 2026
entry.content:
Discover how Franz Regul, former CISO for the Paris 2024 Olympics, tackled unique cybersecurity challenges to protect the Games from evolving threats.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: GlassWorm Malware Evolves to Hide in Dependencies
entry.content:
Researchers have identified dozens of malicious GlassWorm extensions that come with new evasion techniques.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: China-Nexus Hackers Skulk in Southeast Asian Military Orgs for Years
entry.content:
Researchers uncovered an extensive cyber espionage campaign that used novel backdoors and familiar evasion techniques to maintain persistent access to regional targets.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Warlock Ransomware Group Augments Post-Exploitation Activities
entry.content:
In a recent attack, the group showcased stealthier cross-network activity, thanks to its use of a new BYOVD technique and other tools.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Hackers Target Cybersecurity Firm Outpost24 in 7-Stage Phish
entry.content:
In an unsuccessful phishing attack, threat actors leveraged trusted brands and domains to try to redirect a C-suite executive at Outpost24 to give up his credentials.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Less Lucrative Ransomware Market Makes Attackers Alter Methods
entry.content:
Ransomware actors are ditching Cobalt Strike in favor of native Windows tools, as payment rates hit record lows and data theft surges.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: More Attackers Are Logging In, Not Breaking In
entry.content:
Credential theft soared in the second half of 2025, thanks in part to the industrialization of infostealer malware and AI-enabled social engineering.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Oracle EBS Hack: Only 4 Corporate Giants Still Silent on Potential Impact
entry.content:
Broadcom, Bechtel, Estée Lauder, and Abbott Technologies are the only major companies that have yet to issue a public statement. The post Oracle EBS Hack: Only 4 Corporate Giants Still Silent on Potential Impact appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: AI, APIs and DDoS Collide in New Era of Coordinated Cyberattacks
entry.content:
Akamai warns that Layer 7 DDoS, API abuse and AI-powered attacks are merging into coordinated, multi-vector campaigns that are harder to detect and defend against. The post AI, APIs and DDoS Collide in New Era of Coordinated Cyberattacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: CISA Flags Year-Old Wing FTP Vulnerability as Exploited
entry.content:
Tracked as CVE-2025-47813, the flaw leads to the disclosure of the full local installation path of the application. The post CISA Flags Year-Old Wing FTP Vulnerability as Exploited appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Robotic Surgery Giant Intuitive Discloses Cyberattack
entry.content:
The company says some of its internal business applications were accessed after an employee fell victim to a phishing attack. The post Robotic Surgery Giant Intuitive Discloses Cyberattack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Surf AI Raises $57 Million for Agentic Security Operations Platform
entry.content:
The company has announced its launch, backed by funding from Accel, Cyberstarts, and Boldstart Ventures. The post Surf AI Raises $57 Million for Agentic Security Operations Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: UK Companies House Exposed Details of Millions of Firms
entry.content:
The government agency confirmed the vulnerability could have been exploited to obtain company details and alter records. The post UK Companies House Exposed Details of Millions of Firms appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Tech Giants Invest $12.5 Million in Open Source Security
entry.content:
Anthropic, AWS, Google, Microsoft, and OpenAI fund the Linux Foundation’s long-term security initiatives focused on open source software. The post Tech Giants Invest $12.5 Million in Open Source Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Energy Department set to release its first-ever cyber strategy
entry.content:
Alex Fitzsimmons, the acting director of the Office of Cybersecurity, Energy Security, and Emergency Response (CESER), said the plan is meant to supplement the recently-published national cyber strategy and will focus on how the agency will strengthen the “security resilience” of the energy sector.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Georgia man charged for robbing NBA, NFL players through stolen Apple account details
entry.content:
A Georgia man who had already served time for conning professional athletes is accused of impersonating a prominent adult film actress in order to break into the Apple accounts of NBA and NFL players.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Medusa ransomware gang claims attacks on prominent Mississippi hospital, New Jersey county
entry.content:
The Medusa ransomware operation has claimed a devastating cyberattack that knocked out systems at the biggest hospital in Mississippi for nine days.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
entry.content:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
entry.content:
AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
entry.content:
A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Unknown
entry.content:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Proliance Surgeons Settles Data Breach Litigation for $4,450,000
entry.content:
The Seattle, Washington-based surgical group, Proliance Surgeons, has agreed to a settlement to resolve class action litigation over a February […] The post Proliance Surgeons Settles Data Breach Litigation for $4,450,000 appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Iran Linked Hacking Group Wipes Data of U.S. Medical Device Manufacturer
entry.content:
Stryker, a U.S. medical device and medical equipment manufacturer based in Portage, Michigan, is dealing with a cyberattack linked to […] The post Iran Linked Hacking Group Wipes Data of U.S. Medical Device Manufacturer appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Third Ransomware Negotiator Charged Over Involvement with BlackCat Ransomware Group
entry.content:
Another former employee of DigitalMint has been accused of involvement with the ALPHV/Blackcat ransomware group while working as a ransomware […] The post Third Ransomware Negotiator Charged Over Involvement with BlackCat Ransomware Group appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Orthopaedic Institute of Western Kentucky Patients Affected by Vendor Data Breach
entry.content:
Orthopaedic Institute of Western Kentucky has notified patients that their PHI was compromised in two security incidents at their managed […] The post Orthopaedic Institute of Western Kentucky Patients Affected by Vendor Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Long Island Plastic Surgical Group Settles Class Action Lawsuit Over BlackCat Ransomware Attack
entry.content:
A consolidated class action lawsuit against Long Island Plastic Surgical Group, P.C has been resolved with a $2,600,000 settlement. Legal action […] The post Long Island Plastic Surgical Group Settles Class Action Lawsuit Over BlackCat Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: California Dental Care Provider Announces Data Breach
entry.content:
A data breach has been announced by Tieu Dental Corporation in California. The Children’s Council of San Francisco has determined […] The post California Dental Care Provider Announces Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Ransomware Group Claims Attacks on Meadowlark Hills Retirement Community & MedPeds
entry.content:
Meadowlark Hills retirement community in Kansas and MedPeds Associates of Sarasota in Florida have announced data breaches. The Beast ransomware […] The post Ransomware Group Claims Attacks on Meadowlark Hills Retirement Community & MedPeds appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: CommonSpirit Health Patients Affected by Vendor Data Breach
entry.content:
The Chicago, IL-based Catholic health system CommonSpirit Health has announced that it has been affected by a security incident at […] The post CommonSpirit Health Patients Affected by Vendor Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: CISA flags Wing FTP Server flaw as actively exploited in attacks
entry.content:
CISA warned U.S. government agencies to secure their Wing FTP Server instances against an actively exploited vulnerability that may be chained in remote code execution attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Stryker attack wiped tens of thousands of devices, no malware needed
entry.content:
Last week's cyberattack on medical technology giant Stryker was limited to its internal Microsoft environment and remotely wiped tens of thousands of employee devices. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: LeakNet ransomware uses ClickFix, Deno runtime in stealthy attacks
entry.content:
The LeakNet ransomware gang is now using the ClickFix technique for initial access into corporate environments and deploys a malware loader based on the open-source Deno runtime for JavaScript and TypeScript. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Europe sanctions Chinese and Iranian firms for cyberattacks
entry.content:
The European Union Council has announced sanctions against three entities and two individuals for their involvement in cyberattacks targeting critical infrastructure in the region. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.content:
